Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. 2. The bot will almost always only open one PR per version. 3. The bot will stop issuing PRs if more than 3 version bump PRs generated by the bot are open. If you don't want to package a particular version please close the PR. 4. If you want these PRs to be merged automatically, make an issue with @conda-forge-admin,`please add bot automerge` in the title and merge the resulting PR. This command will add our bot automerge feature to your feedstock. 5. If this PR was opened in error or needs to be updated please add the `bot-rerun` label to this PR. The bot will close this PR and schedule another one. If you do not have permissions to add this label, you can use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerun bot in a PR comment to have the `conda-forge-admin` add it for you. While PR #51 apparently updated the feedstock to package `pandera` v0.17.2, more than a month later the version available from `conda-forge` has not been updated and [is still at v0.17.1](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/pandera).

mamba search "pandera>=0.17"
```

```

Hi all, first off thanks for this effort! We use `pandas-stubs` in the [`pandera`](https://pandera.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) project for optional mypy type linting and it's been working great!

Recent changes ([here](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas-stubs/commit/147fbe6a060da0c1acf28ce530e2d39518039f0f)) have caused issues in pandera type annotation, where we use a pandera `SchemaModel` in a subclassed DataFrame class like so:

```python
class MySchema(pandera.SchemaModel): ...

# then you can initialize a validated dataframe like so:
vaildated_data = pandera.typing.DataFrame[MySchema](data)
```

However, this will raise a mypy error if `pandas-stubs` is installed:
```
Type application has too few types (2 expected)
```

**To Reproduce**

Consider this code, which subclasses dataframes :
```python
# bar.py
from typing import Generic, TypeVar

import pandas as pd

T = TypeVar("T")

class MyDataFrame(pd.DataFrame, Generic[T]):
    ...


def func() -> MyDataFrame[int]:
    return MyDataFrame[int]({"foo": [1, 2, 3]})
```

Then using `mypy`:
```
mypy bar.py

# bar.py:14: error: Type application has too few types (2 expected)  [misc]
# Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```

**Please complete the following information:**
 - OS: MacOS
 - OS Version: 10.15.7
 - python version: 3.9.12
 - version of type checker: mypy==0.971
 - version of installed `pandas-stubs`: 1.4.3.220815


**Additional context**

This issue doesn't show up in the previous version `pandas-stubs==1.4.3.220807`

Hi all, first off thanks for this effort! We use `pandas-stubs` in the [`pandera`](https://pandera.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) project for optional mypy type linting and it's been working great!

Recent changes ([here](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas-stubs/commit/147fbe6a060da0c1acf28ce530e2d39518039f0f)) have caused issues in pandera type annotation, where we use a pandera `SchemaModel` in a subclassed DataFrame class like so:

```python
class MySchema(pandera.SchemaModel): ...

# then you can initialize a validated dataframe like so:
vaildated_data = pandera.typing.DataFrame[MySchema](data)
```

However, this will raise a mypy error if `pandas-stubs` is installed:
```
Type application has too few types (2 expected)
```

**To Reproduce**

Consider this code, which subclasses dataframes :
```python
# bar.py
from typing import Generic, TypeVar

import pandas as pd

T = TypeVar("T")

class MyDataFrame(pd.DataFrame, Generic[T]):
    ...


def func() -> MyDataFrame[int]:
    return MyDataFrame[int]({"foo": [1, 2, 3]})
```

Then using `mypy`:
```
mypy bar.py

# bar.py:14: error: Type application has too few types (2 expected)  [misc]
# Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```

I've isolated the cause of this [here](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas-stubs/commit/147fbe6a060da0c1acf28ce530e2d39518039f0f#diff-2a2c7ce81de1d491f11217e36077830686177277dd96884fead786f22b222bf1R166-R170), where removing the `ListLike` type annotations (including the one in the `Iterable` type) makes the error go away. I don't understand why the type annotations in the `__new__` method would effect the type of the subclassed generic arguments 🤷‍♂️

**Please complete the following information:**
 - OS: MacOS
 - OS Version: 10.15.7
 - python version: 3.9.12
 - version of type checker: mypy==0.971
 - version of installed `pandas-stubs`: 1.4.3.220815


**Additional context**

This issue doesn't show up in the previous version `pandas-stubs==1.4.3.220807`

Dependency Analysis -------------------- Please note that this analysis is **highly experimental**. The aim here is to make maintenance easier by inspecting the package's dependencies.
